# servicenow_scheduled_job (Data Source)

`servicenow_scheduled_job` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single scheduled job in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing scheduled job in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_scheduled_job" "example" {
  name = "Example Daily Job"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the scheduled job.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this scheduled job is enabled.
- `condition` (String) Conditional expression that must evaluate true for the job to run.
- `conditional` (Boolean) If true, evaluate the condition before running the job.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `run_dayofmonth` (String) Day of month (1-31) to run the job when run_type is monthly. ServiceNow defaults this to 1 when omitted.
- `run_dayofweek` (String) Day of week (1-7) to run the job when run_type is weekly.
- `run_period` (String) Interval between runs when run_type is periodically (e.g. 1970-01-01 00:00:10).
- `run_start` (String) Start date and time for the schedule. ServiceNow defaults this to the time of creation when omitted.
- `run_time` (String) Time of day to run the job (e.g. 1970-01-01 08:00:00).
- `run_type` (String) How often the job runs. Allowed: daily, weekly, monthly, once, on_demand, business_calendar, periodically.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script` (String) The Javascript code to execute on the schedule.
